summary: Justin discusses capital raising fundamentals for multifamily syndications, focusing on soft commitments, pitch decks, and investor relationship-building. He covers preliminary marketing setup (websites, LLCs, branding) to establish credibility before securing deals, and explains how to collect investor commitments before signing purchase agreements.
takeaways: Soft commitments are collected during pre-deal phase through conversations—investor says 'when you have a deal, send me info' and you add them to CRM
Create a mock/sample pitch deck highlighting tax benefits (bonus depreciation, K-1 offset) to send to soft-committed investors before you have an actual deal
LOI is 'worthless, literally worth the paper it's on'—not legally binding; write 20+ LOIs in 60 days if needed to get first deal
Show brokers 10% of your equity requirement in bank statement or LOI (e.g., $1M property = show $10K-30K down payment proof)—you syndicate the rest
For 30-50 unit deals, target: CAP rate above interest rate environment (currently 6%+), cash-on-cash 6-10% minimum, IRR 16-18%+ ideally
Use A2P registration (federal law) for CRM text messaging; requires LLC with EIN, website, trust center setup in CRM to comply
Partnership on 200+ unit deals: mentee becomes co-GP, receives portion of acquisition fee, gets track record for future larger deals

skill_candidates.change: Implement soft commitment collection system using CRM before acquiring deals
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explains soft commitments are gathered at barbecues/networking by asking interested parties 'when you have a deal, when you have a deal'—collecting names into CRM for future deal pitches

skill_candidates.change: Create sample pitch deck with mock property to show investors before deal is under contract
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin recommends prepping pitch deck with sample returns, property details, and bonus depreciation tax benefits explanation using ChatGPT to attract soft-committed capital

skill_candidates.change: Filter deals using three primary metrics: CAP rate > current interest rate, 6-10% cash-on-cash minimum, 16-18%+ IRR target
skill_candidates.evidence: Dean asks about general deal filters for 30-50 unit properties; Justin responds these three metrics are the main ones to look at, not back-of-napkin rules like 1% or 75% rules

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - Raising Capital Made Simple_09-09-25 | Justin Brennan
